label: Hospitals Damaged
source: Iranian Deputy Health Min. / WHO / OCHA
detail: 31 major hospitals damaged in Iran, 9 rendered non-operational per Day 7 reports, 69+ schools damaged. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure. 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent. 3+ facilities in south Lebanon degraded.

label: Schools Destroyed
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval complex. 165-180 killed (mostly girls aged 7-12). School triple-tapped per Iranian officials. Pentagon investigation underway.

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: Kharg, Abadan, South Pars + Ras Laffan (Qatar) + SAMREF (Saudi) + Kuwait refineries
source: DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: Iran's energy infrastructure: Kharg Island terminal destroyed. South Pars gas field Phases 3/4/5/6 struck Mar 18 — 730M cm³/day field offline. Netanyahu pledged to stop further energy strikes at Trump's demand. IRGC retaliatory strikes: Ras Laffan LNG Trains 4+6 damaged (12.8 MTPA, 3-5yr repair, $20B/yr loss). SAMREF refinery at Yanbu struck. Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries hit. Haifa BAZAN refinery struck. Bahrain Bapco Sitra under force majeure.

label: Airports Damaged
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. Regional airspace closures affect 24,000+ commercial flights.

label: Bridges Destroyed
detail: Road and rail bridges in Kermanshah, Lorestan, and Khuzestan provinces destroyed to degrade IRGC logistics and prevent resupply to missile launch sites.
